findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/Basic.lean:165-178` has `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv` with single/symm-single lemmas; `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MonoidAlgebra.lean:64-85` has `MonoidAlgebra.tensorEquiv` with tensor/single simp lemmas, while this PR reconstructs the same universal-property inverse at lines 107-135.
findings.fix: Define the algebra-equivalence core from `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv` and `MonoidAlgebra.tensorEquiv` (with the needed tensor/order equivalences), then add only the bialgebra compatibility wrapper and generator simp lemmas.
findings.issue: `prodTensorAlgEquiv` hand-rolls the algebra equivalence underlying `R[G × H] ≃ R[G] ⊗ R[H]`, duplicating existing monoid-algebra currying and tensor-base-change machinery.

findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/DiagonalizableGroup.lean:182-193` gives `pointsMulEquiv : WithConv (MonoidAlgebra R G →ₐ[R] A) ≃* (G →* Aˣ)`; `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Prod.lean:460-477` gives `MonoidHom.coprod`, `coprod_comp_inl`, `coprod_comp_inr`, and `coprod_unique`, which decompose `(G' × H' →* Aˣ)` into the two factor character groups.
findings.fix: Build this equivalence by transporting through `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv` on `G' × H'`, `G'`, and `H'`, using `MonoidHom.coprod`/restriction to the factors, or at least prove it agrees with that existing character-level equivalence.
findings.issue: `DiagonalizableGroup.prodPointsMulEquiv` is a parallel diagonalizable-points product API that can already be obtained from the character equivalence and the product universal property for monoid homs.

summary: The PR adds product APIs, but two central constructions overlap existing Mathlib/TauCeti equivalences instead of being expressed through them.
